meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: User Onboarding & Version Control Fixes
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Master user onboarding workflows and implement robust version control using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver elite client projects.
content.answer_block: To solve the absence of version control in your onboarding flows, treat user state as immutable snapshots. By leveraging Claude Code to enforce state-machine logic and Antigravity for rapid scaffolding, you ensure that every onboarding interaction is versioned, reproducible, and recoverable, effectively future-proofing your client’s user experience.
content.intro:
You’re three hours from a client deadline, the onboarding flow is live, and a critical bug just wiped the user's progress. Because you didn't have a version control system integrated into your onboarding logic, you're now manually hot-fixing production code while your client watches the Slack channel. It’s the freelance nightmare: high stakes, zero safety net.

But what if your onboarding wasn't just a series of UI screens, but a versioned engine? By treating your onboarding as infrastructure, you eliminate the risk of 'lost state' and turn a chaotic development process into a repeatable, professional-grade asset that justifies your premium rates.

steps.title: Define the State Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid JSON schema for the onboarding flow to ensure every step is versioned and trackable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a multi-step user onboarding flow that includes a 'version' field and a 'snapshot' object for state persistence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a state-machine schema for onboarding steps in src/types/onboarding.ts'

steps.title: Implement Versioned Middleware
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that checks the schema version before allowing state transitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that validates onboarding state versions and redirects users to a recovery flow if the version mismatch is detected.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add version-check middleware to the Express/Next.js API route /api/onboarding/update'

steps.title: Automate State Snapshots
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject automatic logging every time a user completes an onboarding step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a utility function to save current onboarding progress to a database with a timestamp and schema version identifier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate state-snapshot logging into the current onboarding service'

steps.title: Build the Recovery Controller
steps.description: Develop a controller that pulls the latest valid versioned state if the current session fails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a recovery controller that fetches the latest valid onboarding snapshot from the database when a user session initializes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a recovery controller that handles state restoration based on schema version'

steps.title: CI/CD Integration for Onboarding
steps.description: Ensure your onboarding flow code is automatically linted and tested against the versioned schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs schema validation tests whenever the onboarding service is modified.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure a CI pipeline that validates onboarding schema changes'

pro_tips: Always append a version integer to your state objects; never rely on timestamps alone for sequence tracking.
Use hidden 'ghost' onboarding steps to test new flows in production without impacting legacy users.
Implement a 'rollback' button in your internal admin dashboard specifically for onboarding state corruption.
Always separate your onboarding business logic from your UI components to make version migrations trivial.
Use Claude Code to perform 'diff' analysis between your current onboarding state and the previous version to catch breaking changes before deployment.
resources: State Machine Patterns for SaaS: https://xstate.js.org/docs
content.next_steps: Review your current onboarding architecture, implement the versioning middleware, and audit your production logs for state-loss patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop building disposable code and start shipping resilient, versioned systems that keep your clients coming back for more. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
